You are a senior software/lead developer at crevia. This is the crevia ontext/summaryCrevia Session Summary — April 29, 2026
Context
5 days from the May 4th hard launch. Acting as Senior Software Engineer and Security Architect. Working from the main branch, deploying to https://creviamvp.vercel.app.

What Was Accomplished
1. Critical Security Fix — E2EE Key Backup (DONE, deployed)

The original backup encrypted private keys using the userId as the PBKDF2 password. Since userId is not a secret (it's in every JWT), this meant anyone who obtained the encrypted backup blob from Supabase could decrypt it. Fixed across 5 files:

src/lib/e2e-crypto.ts — Split into v1 (legacy, auto-restore) and v2 (password-based, 210k PBKDF2 iterations). Added isV2Key() versioning, backupPrivateKeyV1 for safe new-user generation, restorePrivateKeyV1 for migration, and the proper backupPrivateKey(password) / restorePrivateKey(userId, password) for v2
src/lib/key-migration.ts — New file. migrateToPasswordScheme() re-encrypts an existing key under a user-chosen password. assessRecoveryPassword() for strength feedback
src/hooks/use-initialize-e2ee.ts — Full new-device guard. Detects v1 vs v2 keys. v1 auto-restores and flags for migration. v2 blocks until user enters recovery password. Emits needsMigration, needsRecoveryPassword, provideRecoveryPassword, clearMigrationFlag
src/hooks/use-e2e-encryption.ts — Fallback restore path updated to be v1/v2 aware. v2 keys return null rather than silently failing
src/hooks/use-subscription.ts — Added Supabase Realtime listener so Pro features unlock the instant a Paystack webhook hits. Fixed isPro to require both creative_pro plan AND active/trialing status — a cancelled Pro user is now correctly treated as Free
2. Recovery Password UI (DONE, deployed)

src/components/auth/RecoveryPasswordModal.tsx — Non-dismissible dialog shown on a new device when a v2 backup exists. Calls provideRecoveryPassword()
src/components/auth/SetRecoveryPasswordDialog.tsx — Dismissible migration prompt with password strength meter. Calls migrateToPasswordScheme() from key-migration.ts
src/App.tsx — Both dialogs wired up. Consumes all new hook flags
3. Auth Page — Terms Enforcement (DONE, deployed)

src/pages/Auth.tsx — Replaced passive terms paragraph with an enforced Checkbox. Create Account button is disabled until checkbox is ticked. Google sign-in also blocked in signup mode if terms not accepted. Fixed broken /terms and /privacy links to correct routes /terms-of-service and /privacy-policy. Improved error messages for all failure cases (wrong password, unconfirmed email, already registered, rate limit)
4. Database Trigger Fix (DONE, applied to production)

supabase/migrations/20260429000000_fix_handle_uniqueness.sql — Fixed handle_new_user trigger. Old version crashed with a UNIQUE constraint violation when the email prefix was already taken as a handle (e.g. same user signed up via Google then tried email/password). New version walks _1, _2, etc. until it finds a free handle. Confirmed applied via pg_proc query
5. Supabase URL Config (DONE)

Added https://creviamvp.vercel.app/** wildcard to Redirect URLs so all post-auth paths are permitted

Blocked / Not Yet Accomplished
1. Email/Password Signup — Supabase SMTP (BLOCKED)

Signup with email/password still returns 500. Root cause: Supabase free tier allows only 3 confirmation emails per hour. Rate limit has been hit from repeated testing. Two options pending:

Quick: Disable "Confirm email" in Supabase Auth settings temporarily
Proper: Configure Resend SMTP (smtp.resend.com, port 465) — needed before launch regardless
2. Recovery Password Flow — Not User-Tested Yet

The code is deployed but has not been tested end-to-end with a real user because signup is blocked by the SMTP issue above.

Remaining Objectives From Original Brief
Feature	Status
Recovery Password (E2EE)	Code complete, UI complete, needs signup unblocked to test
Realtime subscription unlock	Done
Terms enforcement	Done
Lead-to-Contract workflow	Not started
Feature Gating enforcement (server-side)	Not started — client-side limits exist but no server-side RPC guard
Bank Integration (USD)	Not started

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.content:
---
name: Security Architecture — E2EE Key Backup
description: v1 vs v2 key backup scheme, migration path, and key files involved
type: project
---

E2EE private keys are backed up encrypted to Supabase.

**v1 (legacy, broken):** Used userId as the PBKDF2 password — userId is in every JWT so the backup was effectively unencrypted against an attacker with DB access. v1 keys auto-restore and trigger migration.

**v2 (current):** PBKDF2 with 210,000 iterations using a user-chosen recovery password. versioned with isV2Key() check.

Key files:
- `src/lib/e2e-crypto.ts` — crypto primitives, v1/v2 split, backupPrivateKey(password), restorePrivateKey(userId, password), backupPrivateKeyV1, restorePrivateKeyV1
- `src/lib/key-migration.ts` — migrateToPasswordScheme(), assessRecoveryPassword()
- `src/hooks/use-initialize-e2ee.ts` — new-device guard, v1 auto-restore + migration flag, v2 blocks on recovery password. Emits: needsMigration, needsRecoveryPassword, provideRecoveryPassword, clearMigrationFlag
- `src/hooks/use-e2e-encryption.ts` — fallback restore path, v2 returns null on wrong password rather than silently failing
- `src/components/auth/RecoveryPasswordModal.tsx` — non-dismissible, shown when v2 backup exists on new device
- `src/components/auth/SetRecoveryPasswordDialog.tsx` — migration prompt with strength meter

**Why:** Original scheme was a security vulnerability: userId is public, so encrypted backup was trivially decryptable.

**How to apply:** Never use userId or any non-secret as a PBKDF2 password. Any new key storage must go through v2 path.

Everything looks clean. Here's what the fix does:

**Two files changed:**

1. [Auth.tsx](src/pages/Auth.tsx) — Added `hasOAuthCallback()` helper that detects if the URL has a `?code=` (PKCE) or `#access_token=` (implicit flow) from a Google OAuth redirect. When true, `isProcessingOAuth` is set and the login form is replaced with a "Signing you in…" spinner. The `onAuthStateChange` listener navigates to `/kira` on success, or falls back to showing the login form if the exchange fails.

2. [AppLayout.tsx](src/components/navigation/AppLayout.tsx) — Added a `SIGNED_IN` listener as a safety net that re-runs `loadProfile()` in case `getSession()` was called before the session was fully established.

**Before**: After Google sign-in, users were redirected back to `${origin}/auth` (because that was the `redirectTo`), so the login form flashed on screen before the `onAuthStateChange` redirect to `/kira` fired.

**After**: Users land on `/auth` with a spinner that says "Signing you in…" — the form never appears. As soon as the PKCE code exchange completes (< 1 second), `onAuthStateChange` fires and they're sent directly to `/kira`.

For email/password sign-in, nothing changed — the `navigate("/kira")` call in `handleAuth` was already correct.

content.thinking: The user wants me to explain what changes were made to fix the sign-in redirect issue. Let me explain clearly what the problem was and what the fix does.

content.text:
Sure. Let me show you exactly what changed.

**The problem:** When you sign in with Google, here's what was happening:

1. You click "Continue with Google"
2. Google authenticates you
3. Google redirects you back to `/auth` (the login page) — **this is the "back to login" you were seeing**
4. The app then silently redirects you to `/kira` (Kira AI)

So you were literally being sent back to the login page as a middle step, before ending up on Kira. Even if it was brief, you saw the login form again.

---

**What we changed:**

**File 1 — [Auth.tsx](src/pages/Auth.tsx)**

Added a check: when the page loads, does the URL contain an OAuth code (the `?code=` that Google puts in the URL after login)? If yes, instead of showing the login form, show a simple spinner that says **"Signing you in…"**

So now the flow is:
1. You click "Continue with Google"
2. Google authenticates you
3. Google sends you back to `/auth` — but instead of the login form, you see a loading spinner
4. Supabase exchanges the code for a session (~0.5 seconds)
5. You're automatically sent to Kira AI

You never see the login form. Just a brief spinner.

**File 2 — [AppLayout.tsx](src/components/navigation/AppLayout.tsx)**

Added a safety net: if the app layout loaded before your session was fully ready, it now listens for the sign-in event and reloads your profile. This prevents a blank or broken screen in edge cases.

---

**For email/password sign-in** — the code was already correct. After a successful login, `navigate("/kira")` was always being called. If that wasn't working for you, it's likely the Supabase SMTP rate limit issue (signup blocked = can't test new accounts), not the navigation logic.

Found it! The error message "Sorry, I had a little hiccup! Could you try asking me again?" is at line 409 in Kira.tsx. This is in the catch block of the `handleSend` function. So the `streamKiraResponse` is throwing an error.

Let me look at that area to understand what's happening:

```javascript
} catch (error) {
  toast({
    title: "...",
    description: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Couldn't reach Kira right now. Please try again!",
    variant: "destructive",
  });
  content: "Sorry, I had a little hiccup! Could you try asking me again?",
```

I think I found the issue—the CORS allowed origins in the edge function don't include `https://creviamvp.vercel.app`. The function only checks against `https://crevia.app`, `https://www.crevia.app`, `http://localhost:8080`, and `http://localhost:5173`. When a request comes from the Vercel deployment, it won't match any of these, so the CORS header gets set to the fallback origin instead of the actual requesting origin, causing the browser to reject the response.

Let me check if the other edge functions have the same problem and verify the OpenAI API key and RPC configurations are set up correctly. The error message confirms this is a CORS issue—the Vercel preview URL isn't in the allowed origins list. I need to add `https://creviamvp.vercel.app` to `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` in the kira-gpt function, and I should check the other Kira functions for the same problem.

Though wait, let me think through what happens when `supabase.functions.invoke` fails. If the function returns a non-2xx status, it returns an error object that gets thrown in the catch block. So whether it's a CORS error or a missing environment variable, the behavior would be similar—but a CORS error would actually prevent the browser from reading the response at all, whereas a 500 from missing credentials would at least reach the client.

The most likely culprits are CORS configuration (since `creviamvp.vercel.app` might not be in the allowed origins), a missing or invalid OpenAI API key, or some other environment setup issue. Let me start by checking the CORS settings.

Actually, I'm also noticing a potential inconsistency in how the profile data is being queried. I'm looking at the client-side code in Kira.tsx where it's fetching `kira_actions_today` and `kira_actions_limit`...

Checking the server-side edge function...

The client and server are checking different column names — the client uses `kira_actions_today` and `kira_actions_limit`, while the server uses `kira_tokens_used_today` and `kira_tokens_limit_daily`. These might be separate columns entirely, which could explain why the limits aren't being enforced consistently.

If those client-side columns don't exist, they'd default to 0 and 10 respectively, so the check would pass unless the user has already hit 10 actions. Meanwhile, the server could be hitting its own limit and returning a reply message instead of throwing an error. The real issue is probably the CORS configuration — I need to add the Vercel app domain to the allowed origins in the edge function.

Looking at the edge function flow, it validates the token, checks rate limits, then calls OpenAI. If the OpenAI call fails with an invalid key, it returns a 500 error that the client interprets as a CORS error, which would explain the "hiccup" message the user is seeing.

Since I can't directly check the browser console or function logs, I'll start by fixing the most obvious issue: adding the correct production domain `https://creviamvp.vercel.app` to the CORS allowed origins in the edge function. I should also verify all other edge functions have the same CORS configuration to prevent similar issues elsewhere.

content.thinking:
I see the issue. After signup, the code does:
1. Shows a toast: "Check your email"
2. Checks MFA level
3. Navigates to `/kira`

BUT - the problem is on line 175: `emailRedirectTo: \`${window.location.origin}/user-type-selection\``

When the user clicks the confirmation link in their email, they get redirected to `/user-type-selection`. But if email confirmation is disabled (which is the case since they're hitting rate limits), Supabase won't send a confirmation email and will automatically log the user in. In that case, it navigates directly to `/kira` (line 211).


The real issue is that when email confirmation is enabled, the `signUp` call doesn't actually authenticate the user—it just registers the account and sends a confirmation email. So the code navigates to `/kira` on line 211 even though there's no active session yet, and the route guard immediately bounces them back to `/auth` since they're not authenticated.

The solution is to check the `session` field in the response from `supabase.auth.signUp`. If `session` is null, email confirmation is required and we should show a message telling them to check their email instead of navigating away. If `session` exists, the user is auto-confirmed and we can proceed to `/kira`.

content.text:
Found the problem. On line 171, after signup the code navigates to `/kira` (line 211) regardless — but if Supabase requires email confirmation, the user isn't actually logged in yet. So they land on `/kira`, the route guard sees no session, and bounces them back to `/auth`.

The fix: check whether signup returned a live session. If it did (auto-confirm or confirmation disabled), go to Kira. If not (email pending), stay on the page with the "check your email" toast.

.tsx** to properly handle the signup → immediate session path (already done in my edit)

Here's your commit message:

```
fix: production-ready auth flow — route guards, signup redirect, CORS

  /crevia-link, /crevia-studio, /crevia-workspace, /profile/*). Unauthenticated
  users are now redirected to /auth instead of seeing a broken guest state.

- Fix signup flow: only navigate to /kira when Supabase returns a live session
  (email confirmation disabled). If confirmation is required, stay on /auth and
  show the check-your-email toast — no more premature redirect to /kira with no session.

- Fix emailRedirectTo from /user-type-selection (which redirected to /auth,
  causing a visible bounce) to /kira so confirmation link lands users directly
  in the app.

- Add creviamvp.vercel.app to ALLOWED_ORIGINS in all 7 edge functions and
  redeploy — fixes the "hiccup" error that blocked every Kira message.
```

**One more thing you need to do in the Supabase dashboard** (can't be done via code):

Go to **Supabase → Authentication → Providers → Email** and turn off **"Confirm email"**. This means after signup, the user gets a live session immediately and lands on Kira with no extra steps. Without this toggle, every new email signup still has to wait for a confirmation email before they can use the app — and your SMTP is still rate-limited.
explain for me well while i should turn it off

content.thinking: The user wants me to explain why they should turn off "Confirm email" in Supabase. Let me give a clear, honest explanation of the tradeoffs.

content.text:
Here's the honest breakdown:

---

**What "Confirm email" does**

When it's ON, after a user signs up, Supabase does NOT create a live session. Instead it sends them an email with a link. Only after they click that link does their account become active and usable. Until then, they can't log in, can't use Kira, can't do anything.

---

**Why it's blocking you right now**

Supabase's free tier only allows **3 confirmation emails per hour**. You've already hit that limit from testing. So right now, if someone signs up:

1. The signup appears to succeed (no error shown)
2. Supabase tries to send the confirmation email — silently fails because of the rate limit
3. The user never receives an email
4. Their account exists in the database but is permanently locked out
5. They can't log in, can't recover, stuck forever

That's a worse experience than no email confirmation at all.

---

**Why turning it OFF is the right call for now**

With it OFF:

- User fills in the form → hits "Create Account" → gets a live session immediately → lands on Kira
- Zero friction, zero waiting, zero chance of being locked out
- No dependency on email delivery at all

You're 5 days from launch. You don't have SMTP configured yet (Resend setup is still pending). Leaving it ON with broken SMTP means **every new signup is a dead account**.

---

**The security tradeoff**

The only thing you lose by turning it OFF is the ability to verify that the user actually owns the email address they typed. Someone could sign up with `fake@gmail.com` and it would work. In practice for a creative platform like Crevia, this is a very low risk — you're not a bank. Most real users want to access the product, not fake their identity.

---

**The proper long-term plan**

Turn it OFF now → launch → set up Resend SMTP → turn it back ON once email delivery is reliable. That's the correct sequence. Running with broken email confirmation is worse than no confirmation at all.
i think b4 making any change i should discuss wit my team members first

That's the right call. No rush — this is a config change that affects every new user, so alignment with the team first makes sense.

When you come back to it, the options on the table are:

1. **Turn off email confirmation** (Supabase dashboard) — zero friction, works immediately, revisit once SMTP is set up
2. **Set up Resend SMTP** — proper fix, keeps email verification, takes about 30 minutes to configure

The code changes we made today (route guards, signup redirect, CORS fix on all 7 edge functions) are all ready to push whenever you're ready. None of them depend on the Supabase email confirmation decision.

content.text: and what if i pay for supabase pro

content.thinking: The user is asking about paying for Supabase Pro and how it would affect the email confirmation situation.

content.text:
Good question. Here's what Supabase Pro actually gives you on email:

**Free tier:** 3 confirmation emails per hour (shared Supabase SMTP)

**Pro tier ($25/month):** The rate limit goes up, but Supabase still recommends you bring your own SMTP for production. Their built-in email sender is not designed for production reliability — no delivery guarantees, no bounce handling, emails can land in spam.

---

**So paying for Pro alone doesn't fully solve it.**

The proper production setup regardless of plan is:

1. Pay for Pro (removes the 3/hr rate limit for testing)
2. Connect your own SMTP via Resend (reliable delivery, custom `from` address like `noreply@crevia.app`, delivery tracking)

Resend's free tier handles **3,000 emails/month** which is more than enough for your launch. Setup takes about 20-30 minutes — you add a DNS record to your domain, get an API key, and paste the SMTP credentials into Supabase Auth settings.

---

**My honest recommendation for your situation:**

- If you're going Pro anyway for other reasons (database size, more API calls, better support) — go for it
- But don't pay for Pro *just* to fix the email rate limit. Setting up Resend on the free tier solves it better and for free
- Either way, Resend SMTP is the piece you actually need configured before launch

content.text:
Done reading. Here's exactly what I found — no changes made yet.

---

**There are 3 issues in the current code:**

---

**Issue 1 — ProtectedRoute can bounce authenticated users (the main culprit)**

[ProtectedRoute.tsx:15](src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx#L15) only uses `onAuthStateChange`. That event fires asynchronously. If your access token is expired and Supabase needs to silently refresh it, `INITIAL_SESSION` fires with `null` first, then fires again with the refreshed session. During that null window, ProtectedRoute immediately redirects you to `/auth` — even though you ARE logged in. That's the "taken back to login" you're seeing.

The original `AppLayout` used `getSession()` directly, which handles token refresh internally before returning. That's why it worked before.

---

**Issue 2 — Signup stays on `/auth` when email confirmation is ON**

[Auth.tsx:205](src/pages/Auth.tsx#L205) — after signup, the code only navigates to Kira `if (signUpData.session)`. If email confirmation is enabled in Supabase, that session is always `null` after signup, so the user never gets redirected — they stay on the login page. This is technically correct behaviour, but if Resend isn't fully wired up yet, users never receive the confirmation email and are permanently stuck.

---

**Issue 3 — Sign-in has two competing navigates**

When signing in with email/password:
- [Auth.tsx:238](src/pages/Auth.tsx#L238) — `handleAuth` calls `navigate("/kira")` directly
- [Auth.tsx:74](src/pages/Auth.tsx#L74) — `onAuthStateChange` ALSO calls `navigate("/kira")` on `SIGNED_IN`

Two navigates to the same place. Usually harmless, but it creates a race condition on slower connections.

---

**What I recommend fixing (in order of priority):**

1. **ProtectedRoute** — restore `getSession()` as the primary check, keep `onAuthStateChange` as the live listener. That's the fix for the bounce.
2. **Sign-in** — remove the duplicate navigate from `handleAuth` since `onAuthStateChange` already handles it.
3. **Signup** — confirm with me: is email confirmation **ON or OFF** in your Supabase dashboard right now? That determines whether Issue 2 is actually a problem.

Want me to go ahead with all three fixes?

content.text: Those are debug logs from the E2EE hook in App.tsx. Let me find them.

Nothing wrong — this is expected behaviour for a logged-out user. Here's what each line means:

---

**`[E2EE] AppContent render — userId: (not yet set)`**
The app just loaded. React rendered `AppContent` for the first time. `userId` is still `""` because `onAuthStateChange` hasn't fired yet.

**`[E2EE] Auth state changed — userId: (signed out)`** *(first time)*
Supabase fired the `INITIAL_SESSION` event — this always fires immediately when you subscribe to `onAuthStateChange`. Since no one is logged in, `session` is null → logs "signed out".

**`[E2EE] Auth state changed — userId: (signed out)`** *(second time)*
This is likely the `SIGNED_OUT` event firing, which Supabase sends when it confirms there's no active session to refresh. Completely normal.

---

**In short: you're looking at the app loading with no user logged in. These logs are just the E2EE system confirming "no user yet".** Once someone signs in you'd see:
```
[E2EE] Auth state changed — userId: abc123-real-uuid
```

The logs were added during the E2EE security work as debugging aids. They're harmless but should be removed before launch. Want me to strip them out now?

Now I have the full picture. Let me identify all the issues:

**Issue 1: ProtectedRoute race condition**

Current ProtectedRoute uses BOTH `getSession()` AND `onAuthStateChange`. The problem:
- `getSession()` is async (promise-based)
- `onAuthStateChange` fires `INITIAL_SESSION` asynchronously (via setTimeout in Supabase v2)
- Both fire at roughly the same time
- If they disagree (one says auth, other says unauth), the last one to fire wins
- If INITIAL_SESSION fires with null (before session is established) and getSession hasn't resolved yet, we might briefly set "unauth" and redirect


- The fix: Use `getSession()` only for initial state detection, and reserve `onAuthStateChange` for explicit transitions like SIGNED_IN, SIGNED_OUT, and TOKEN_REFRESHED.

**Issue 2: Auth.tsx - signup with email confirmation ON stays on login**

When a user signs up with email confirmation enabled, `signUpData.session` is null, so they stay on the login page with a toast notification instead of being guided to check their email. This leaves them confused and stuck when they later try to log in and hit the "Email not confirmed" error. I need to show a dedicated confirmation screen instead.

**Issue 3: App.tsx debug console.logs**

Those debug logs are cluttering the console and should be removed since they were just temporary debugging aids.

**Issue 4: Auth.tsx - potential race condition with `onAuthStateChange`**

The `onAuthStateChange` listener fires on INITIAL_SESSION with a null session, but the condition `event === 'SIGNED_IN' && session` correctly filters this out. However, there's a timing issue: when `signInWithPassword` resolves, the navigation now depends entirely on the `onAuthStateChange` SIGNED_IN event firing, but there could be a delay between the sign-in completing and that listener triggering. content.text:
**Frontend-driven auth, with Supabase as the backend service.**

Here's exactly what that means for Crevia:

**What happens on the frontend (your code):**
- `Auth.tsx` collects credentials and calls Supabase SDK methods (`signUp`, `signInWithPassword`, `signInWithOAuth`)
- `ProtectedRoute.tsx` checks for a session via `getSession()` and redirects if none exists
- The session token is stored in **localStorage** in the browser
- `onAuthStateChange` listens for session changes client-side

**What Supabase handles (their servers):**
- Verifying passwords, issuing JWTs, refreshing tokens
- Google OAuth handshake
- Sending confirmation emails (via Resend SMTP you configured)
- Storing users in the `auth.users` table

**What this means practically:**
- Route protection is enforced on the client — a determined user could technically bypass `ProtectedRoute` by manipulating localStorage, but they still couldn't call your edge functions because those validate the JWT server-side
- Your edge functions (`kira-gpt`, etc.) do their own JWT verification using `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, so the real data is protected even if someone bypasses the UI

**The short answer:** Auth decisions happen on the frontend, auth verification happens on Supabase's servers. This is the standard setup for a Supabase + React SPA — nothing wrong with it.

The session token flow in this app:
1. User signs in → Supabase issues a JWT (JSON Web Token)
2. JWT is stored in localStorage as `sb-bcbzixdcsigxhqdiveei-auth-token` (or similar)
3. On page load, Supabase client reads from localStorage to restore the session
4. The JWT has an expiry (typically 1 hour) and is auto-refreshed using a refresh token


5. For new users specifically, email confirmation is required before any session token gets issued, which is why they're stuck at sign-in until they verify their email.

content.text:
Yes, directly. Let me explain exactly what's happening.

**How session tokens work in this app:**

When a user signs in successfully, Supabase issues two tokens stored in `localStorage`:
- **Access token** (JWT) — expires after 1 hour, used to authenticate API calls
- **Refresh token** — long-lived, used to silently get a new access token when the hour is up

```
localStorage → sb-bcbzixdcsigxhqdiveei-auth-token
```

---

**Why new user sign-in has been hard — the real root cause:**

For a **new user signing up**, with email confirmation ON:

```
signUp() called
    ↓
Supabase creates the account
    ↓
NO session token issued yet  ← this is the problem
    ↓
signUpData.session = null
    ↓
Nothing in localStorage
    ↓
ProtectedRoute calls getSession() → gets null → bounces to /auth
```

The session token is only issued **after** the user clicks the confirmation email. Until that moment, the user exists in the database but has no token — so every protected page treats them as a stranger.

---

**For an existing user signing in**, the token flow is clean:

```
signInWithPassword() called
    ↓
Supabase validates credentials
    ↓
Access token + refresh token written to localStorage immediately
    ↓
getSession() finds it → "auth" → Kira loads
```

---

**The fix we already applied handles this correctly:**

- New signup → "Check your inbox" screen (no redirect attempt since there's no token)
- Email confirmed → token issued → `SIGNED_IN` fires → Kira
- `ProtectedRoute` now uses `getSession()` first which reads localStorage directly — no race condition

The remaining question is purely about your Supabase config: **is Resend delivering the confirmation emails?** If yes, the full token flow works. If no, new users never get their token and stay locked out.

Want to test it right now with a real email?

**Two ways to fix it:**

**Option 1 — Turn off email confirmation** *(2 minutes, do it right now)*
Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Providers → Email → toggle **"Confirm email" OFF**

After signup, Supabase issues the session token immediately. User goes straight to Kira. Done.

**Option 2 — Keep it ON but verify Resend is working** *(only if you want email verification)*
Create a brand new account with a real email you can check right now. If the confirmation email arrives within 60 seconds, the flow works. If it doesn't arrive, Resend isn't connected properly and new users are locked out.

content.thinking:
The user is seeing "Database error saving new user" from Supabase. This is a Supabase-side error, not a frontend error. Let me explain what this means.

"Database error saving new user" is a well-known Supabase error that occurs when the `handle_new_user` trigger on the `auth.users` table fails. This trigger runs whenever a new user is created and tries to insert a row into the `public.profiles` table.

Looking at the git history, there was a migration `20260429000000_fix_handle_uniqueness.sql` that fixed a UNIQUE constraint violation in the handle_new_user trigger. This was mentioned in the session summary as "Fixed handle_new_user trigger. 

The error happens because Supabase Auth successfully creates the user in `auth.users`, but then the `handle_new_user` trigger fails when trying to create the corresponding profiles row, causing the entire operation to roll back. The most likely culprit is either that the uniqueness fix migration wasn't applied to production, or there's a NOT NULL constraint in the profiles table that the trigger isn't satisfying.

content.text: This is not a frontend error at all. Let me show you exactly what's happening.

content.content:
1	-- Fix handle_new_user trigger to gracefully handle duplicate handles.
2	-- The old version would crash with a unique constraint violation if the email
3	-- prefix was already taken (e.g. user signs up via Google then tries email/password).
4	-- Now it appends _1, _2, etc. until it finds a free handle.
5	
6	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
7	RETURNS trigger
8	LANGUAGE plpgsql
9	SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
10	AS $$
11	DECLARE
12	  base_handle TEXT;
13	  final_handle TEXT;
14	  counter INTEGER := 0;
15	BEGIN
16	  -- Sanitise: lowercase, replace dots/special chars with underscores
17	  base_handle := LOWER(
18	    REGEXP_REPLACE(
19	      COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_metadata->>'handle', SPLIT_PART(NEW.email, '@', 1)),
20	      '[^a-z0-9_]', '_', 'g'
21	    )
22	  );
23	
24	  final_handle := base_handle;
25	
26	  -- Walk until we find a handle that isn't taken
27	  WHILE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE handle = final_handle) LOOP
28	    counter := counter + 1;
29	    final_handle := base_handle || '_' || counter;
30	  END LOOP;
31	
32	  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, user_type, handle, email)
33	  VALUES (
34	    NEW.id,
35	    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_metadata->>'user_type', 'creator')::public.user_type,
36	    final_handle,
37	    NEW.email
38	  );
39	
40	  RETURN NEW;
41	END;
42	$$;
43

content.content:
-- ─── notifications ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Stores per-user in-app notifications. Triggered by DB-level events so
-- delivery is reliable regardless of which client initiated the action.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.notifications (
  id         uuid        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id    uuid        NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type       text        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system',
  title      text        NOT NULL,
  body       text,
  data       jsonb       NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
  read       boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id
  ON public.notifications(user_id);

-- Partial index speeds up the unread-count query significantly.
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread
  ON public.notifications(user_id, created_at DESC)
  WHERE read = false;

ALTER TABLE public.notifications ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "notif_select_own" ON public.notifications;
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "notif_update_own" ON public.notifications;

-- Users may only see their own notifications.
CREATE POLICY "notif_select_own"
  ON public.notifications FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (user_id = auth.uid());

-- Users may mark their own notifications as read (only the read column matters).
CREATE POLICY "notif_update_own"
  ON public.notifications FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (user_id = auth.uid())
  WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid());

-- No user-level INSERT policy — only SECURITY DEFINER trigger functions write here.


-- ─── Trigger: new chat message ────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Notifies all room members (except the sender) when a message is sent.
-- Messages from the same room are grouped into a single unread notification
-- for 30 minutes to prevent notification spam in fast-moving chats.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_chat_message()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_member_id   uuid;
  v_sender_name text;
  v_existing_id uuid;
BEGIN
  -- Skip system / voiceNote / file messages — only text gets a push.
  IF NEW.message_type IS NOT NULL AND NEW.message_type != 'text' THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  SELECT COALESCE(display_name, handle, 'Someone')
  INTO   v_sender_name
  FROM   public.profiles
  WHERE  id = NEW.sender_id;

  FOR v_member_id IN
    SELECT user_id
    FROM   public.chat_room_members
    WHERE  room_id = NEW.room_id
      AND  user_id != NEW.sender_id
  LOOP
    -- Look for an existing unread notification for the same room within 30 min.
    SELECT id INTO v_existing_id
    FROM   public.notifications
    WHERE  user_id = v_member_id
      AND  type    = 'message'
      AND  (data->>'room_id') = NEW.room_id::text
      AND  read    = false
      AND  created_at > now() - interval '30 minutes'
    LIMIT 1;

    IF v_existing_id IS NOT NULL THEN
      -- Bump the existing notification so it surfaces at the top.
      UPDATE public.notifications
      SET    title      = v_sender_name || ' sent more messages',
             created_at = now()
      WHERE  id = v_existing_id;
    ELSE
      INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
      VALUES (
        v_member_id,
        'message',
        v_sender_name || ' sent you a message',
        CASE
          WHEN NEW.is_encrypted THEN '🔒 Encrypted message'
          ELSE LEFT(COALESCE(NEW.content, ''), 120)
        END,
        jsonb_build_object('room_id', NEW.room_id::text)
      );
    END IF;
  END LOOP;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_chat_message ON public.chat_messages;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_chat_message
  AFTER INSERT ON public.chat_messages
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_chat_message();


-- ─── Trigger: contract status change ─────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_contract_status()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  IF NEW.status IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
    INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
    SELECT
      p.id,
      'contract',
      CASE NEW.status
        WHEN 'signed'   THEN 'Contract signed'
        WHEN 'expired'  THEN 'Contract expired'
        ELSE                 'Contract updated'
      END,
      COALESCE(NEW.title, 'Untitled contract'),
      jsonb_build_object('contract_id', NEW.id::text)
    FROM public.profiles p
    WHERE p.id = NEW.user_id
       OR p.id = NEW.client_id
    ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_contract_status ON public.contracts;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_contract_status
  AFTER UPDATE ON public.contracts
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_contract_status();


-- ─── Trigger: invoice status change ──────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.notify_invoice_status()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  IF NEW.status IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
    INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
    SELECT
      p.id,
      'invoice',
      CASE NEW.status
        WHEN 'paid'     THEN 'Invoice paid'
        WHEN 'overdue'  THEN 'Invoice overdue'
        ELSE                 'Invoice updated'
      END,
      COALESCE(NEW.invoice_number, 'Invoice') || ' — ' ||
        to_char(COALESCE(NEW.total, 0), 'FM999,999,990.00'),
      jsonb_build_object('invoice_id', NEW.id::text)
    FROM public.profiles p
    WHERE p.id = NEW.user_id
    ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_invoice_status ON public.invoices;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_invoice_status
  AFTER UPDATE ON public.invoices
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_invoice_status();

AS $$
BEGIN
  IF NEW.status IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
    INSERT INTO public.notifications (user_id, type, title, body, data)
    SELECT
      p.id,
      'invoice',
      CASE NEW.status
        WHEN 'paid'     THEN 'Invoice paid'
        WHEN 'overdue'  THEN 'Invoice overdue'
        ELSE                 'Invoice updated'
      END,
      COALESCE(NEW.invoice_number, 'Invoice') || ' — ' ||
        to_char(COALESCE(NEW.total, 0), 'FM999,999,990.00'),
      jsonb_build_object('invoice_id', NEW.id::text)
    FROM public.profiles p
    WHERE p.id = NEW.user_id
    ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_notify_invoice_status ON public.invoices;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_notify_invoice_status
  AFTER UPDATE ON public.invoices
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.notify_invoice_status();

content.content:
-- FIX 1: Chat room hijacking - Remove the OR branch that lets any user join any room
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Room creators can add members" ON public.chat_room_members;
CREATE POLICY "Room creators can add members"
ON public.chat_room_members
FOR INSERT
TO authenticated
WITH CHECK (
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM chat_rooms
    WHERE chat_rooms.id = chat_room_members.room_id
    AND chat_rooms.created_by = auth.uid()
  )
);

-- FIX 2: Restrict is_room_member to only allow checking own membership
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_room_member(_room_id uuid, _user_id uuid)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = 'public'
AS $$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.chat_room_members
    WHERE room_id = _room_id AND user_id = _user_id
  )
$$;

-- FIX 3: Fix email exposure - create a public view excluding sensitive data
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.profiles_public
WITH (security_invoker = on) AS
SELECT id, user_type, handle, display_name, bio, avatar_url, is_verified, 
       verification_status, verification_method, created_at, updated_at
FROM public.profiles;

-- FIX 4: Fix function search path on update_updated_at_column
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at_column()
RETURNS trigger
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY INVOKER
SET search_path = 'public'
AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

Now I have the full picture. The profiles table was originally defined with just these columns:
- `id` - NOT NULL (PRIMARY KEY)

- `display_name` - nullable
- `bio` - nullable
- `avatar_url` - nullable
- `verification_status` - DEFAULT 'pending'
- `verification_method` - nullable
- `is_verified` - DEFAULT FALSE
- `created_at` - DEFAULT now()
- `updated_at` - DEFAULT now()

The trigger inserts: `(id, user_type, handle, email)` - this covers all NOT NULL columns.

content.content:
-- Fix the handle_new_user function to use correct field name
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS trigger
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, user_type, handle, email)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'user_type', 'creator')::public.user_type,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'handle', SPLIT_PART(NEW.email, '@', 1)),
    NEW.email
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$;

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Here's exactly what's happening — no guessing.

---

**The error "Database error saving new user" has nothing to do with the frontend.**

It means the **`handle_new_user` database trigger is crashing** the moment you try to create an account. Here's the exact flow:

```
User clicks "Create Account"
    ↓
Supabase creates a row in auth.users ✓
    ↓
handle_new_user trigger fires automatically
    ↓
Trigger tries to INSERT a row into public.profiles
    ↓
INSERT fails with a database error
    ↓
Supabase rolls back EVERYTHING including the user
    ↓
Returns "Database error saving new user" to the frontend
    ↓
Your console logs: "Signup error: Database error saving new user"
```

---

**Why is the trigger failing?** Two possible causes I found:

**Cause 1 — Column name mismatch between trigger and the live database.**
The trigger in production was written using `raw_user_meta_data` (old name). The fix migration `20260429000000` rewrites it using `raw_user_metadata` (new name). If that migration never actually ran in production, the old trigger is still there — and failing silently on certain signups.

**Cause 2 — Columns exist in your live profiles table that aren't in any migration.**
Your Kira page queries `kira_actions_today` and `kira_actions_limit`. Your edge function queries `kira_tokens_used_today` and `kira_tokens_limit_daily`. But **none of these column names appear in any migration file** — they were added directly via the Supabase dashboard. If any of them were added as `NOT NULL` without a `DEFAULT`, the trigger's INSERT fails because it doesn't provide values for those columns.

---

**How to confirm which one it is:**

Go to your **Supabase dashboard → Table Editor → profiles → columns**. Look for any column that says `NOT NULL` and has no default value set. That's your culprit.

Then tell me which columns you find and I'll write the exact fix migration right now.

content.text:
I now have all the data needed to give you a definitive answer. Stop — let me explain before touching anything.

---

**The root cause is NOT in the database.**

Here's what the investigation found:

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `handle_new_user` function in production | ✓ Correct (our fix is live) |
| Trigger attached to `auth.users` | ✓ Attached |
| All profiles columns have defaults | ✓ None are blocking |
| `postgres` role bypasses RLS | ✓ `rolbypassrls = true` |
| Orphaned users (auth.users without profiles) | ✗ Zero — meaning the transaction rolls back entirely |
| Users created via email/password signup | ✗ Zero — no email signup has ever succeeded |

That last line is the key. **Zero email/password users exist.** Google OAuth users exist fine. This means the error is happening before our trigger even runs — inside Supabase Auth's own code.

---

**The real cause: Resend SMTP is failing during signup.**

When email confirmation is ON, Supabase tries to send a confirmation email as part of the signup transaction. In newer Supabase versions, if the SMTP call fails (wrong credentials, wrong host, not verified in Resend), the entire signup is rolled back and Supabase returns `"Database error saving new user"` — not an SMTP error, which makes it look like a database problem.

Google OAuth works because it never triggers the email send.

---

**No migration can fix this.** This is a Supabase dashboard configuration issue.

**You have two options — pick one right now:**

**Option A (2 minutes):** Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Providers → Email → toggle **"Confirm email" OFF**. Users get a session immediately. No email needed. Everything works instantly.

**Option B (10 minutes):** Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → Auth → SMTP Settings — verify your Resend credentials are saved correctly: host `smtp.resend.com`, port `465`, username `resend`, password is your Resend API key. Then send a test email from the Resend dashboard to confirm delivery is working.

file.content:
---
name: Remaining Work Before Launch
description: Feature gaps that must be addressed before the 2026-05-04 hard launch
type: project
originSessionId: 5575c1c2-2ae2-40d7-b4ef-59c8f88d8717
---
As of 2026-04-29, these items are not yet done:

1. **SMTP fix** (BLOCKER) — Email/password signup is broken. Supabase free tier rate-limited. Fix: configure Resend SMTP (smtp.resend.com, port 465) in Supabase Auth settings, or temporarily disable email confirmation to unblock testing.

2. **Recovery password E2E test** — Blocked by signup. Once SMTP is fixed, must test full new-device + recovery password flow end-to-end with a real user.

3. **Lead-to-Contract workflow** — Not started.

4. **Server-side feature gating** — Client-side limits exist but no server-side RPC guard. A determined user could bypass client checks. Needs a Postgres RPC or edge function that enforces plan limits before writing.

5. **Bank Integration (USD)** — Not started.

**Why:** Hard launch 2026-05-04. Items 1 and 4 are highest risk; 3 and 5 are product features.

**How to apply:** When the user asks what to work on next, surface SMTP fix first, then server-side gating, then the product features.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user (Anthony Onyango, Senior Software Engineer at Crevia) has been working toward a hard launch of Crevia MVP at https://creviamvp.vercel.app on May 4, 2026. The session covered multiple issues in priority order: fixing the auth redirect loop on sign-in/signup, fixing Kira AI's "hiccup" error, hardening the authentication architecture with a centralized ProtectedRoute, removing legacy auth bouncers from individual pages, and diagnosing a persistent "Database error saving new user" signup failure. The final message was a request for a full context summary of what was done and what remains. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Supabase Auth (email/password + Google OAuth / PKCE flow), session tokens, localStorage persistence - ProtectedRoute pattern — centralized auth guard vs. per-page manual redirects - onAuthStateChange vs. getSession() race conditions (INITIAL_SESSION timing) - Email confirmation flow: signUpData.session = null when ON, token_hash in URL on confirmation link click - CORS: ALLOWED_ORIGINS in Supabase edge functions - handle_new_user PostgreSQL trigger on auth.users (AFTER INSERT, SECURITY DEFINER) - Supabase SMTP: free tier rate-limit (3/hr), Resend custom SMTP (smtp.resend.com port 465) - E2EE key backup v1 (userId as PBKDF2 password — insecure) vs. v2 (user recovery password, 210k iterations) - Supabase Realtime subscriptions for Pro feature unlocking - Vercel auto-deploy on push to main branch - RLS (Row Level Security), rolbypassrls, FORCE ROW SECURITY - SECURITY DEFINER functions and their interaction with RLS - JWT / access token + refresh token lifecycle 3. Files and Code Sections: - **src/pages/Auth.tsx** — Core auth page. Most heavily modified file in the session. - Added `hasOAuthCallback()` function detecting `code`, `token_hash`, or `access_token=` in URL - Added `isProcessingOAuth` state: shows loading spinner instead of login form during OAuth/email-confirmation token exchange - Added `emailConfirmPending` and `pendingEmail` state variables - After signup: checks `signUpData.session` — if session exists navigate to `/kira`, if null show dedicated "Check your inbox" screen - emailRedirectTo corrected to `${window.location.origin}/auth` - Removed duplicate `navigate("/kira")` from sign-in handleAuth path (relies on onAuthStateChange SIGNED_IN) - useEffect: getSession() redirects already-authed users; onAuthStateChange SIGNED_IN navigates to /kira - Dedicated email confirmation pending screen: ```tsx if (emailConfirmPending) { return (

Check your inbox

We sent a confirmation link to
{pendingEmail}

Click the link in your email to activate your account and access Kira. Check your spam folder if you don't see it.
); } ``` - **src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx** — New file created. Wraps all protected routes. Final version: ```tsx const ProtectedRoute = ({ children }: ProtectedRouteProps) => { const [status, setStatus] = useState<"loading" | "auth" | "unauth">("loading"); useEffect(() => { // getSession() is the single authoritative initial check supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => { setStatus(session ? "auth" : "unauth"); }); // onAuthStateChange handles live transitions only (not INITIAL_SESSION) const { data: { subscription } } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { if (event === "SIGNED_IN" || event === "TOKEN_REFRESHED") { setStatus("auth"); } else if (event === "SIGNED_OUT") { setStatus("unauth"); } }); return () => subscription.unsubscribe(); }, []); if (status === "loading") return ; if (status === "unauth") return ; return <>{children}; }; ``` - **src/App.tsx** — All protected routes wrapped with ProtectedRoute. Debug console.logs removed. Import added for ProtectedRoute. ```tsx } /> } /> // ...same pattern for all protected routes ``` - Removed: `console.log("[E2EE] AppContent render — userId:", userId || "(not yet set)");` - Simplified: `setUserId(session?.user?.id ?? "");` - **src/components/navigation/AppLayout.tsx** — Added onAuthStateChange listener to reload profile on SIGNED_IN event (for OAuth PKCE callback timing). - **src/pages/profile/PaymentsBilling.tsx** — Removed `if (!session) { navigate("/auth"); return; }` and `useNavigate` import. - **src/pages/profile/Verification.tsx** — Removed `if (!session) { navigate("/auth"); return; }` and `useNavigate` import. - **src/pages/profile/Settings.tsx** — Removed `if (!session) { navigate("/auth"); return; }` (kept useNavigate for other uses at lines 87, 124). - **src/pages/CreviaLink.tsx** — Removed `if (!session) { navigate("/auth"); return; }` at line 71. Kept useNavigate for button: `navigate(\`/crevia-studio?tab=link§ion=${tab.id}\`)` at line 577. - **src/pages/Pricing.tsx** — Deliberately left unchanged. `navigate("/auth")` at line 30 is inside `handleUpgrade()` button handler, not a page-load bouncer. Correct to keep. - **src/pages/onboarding/KiraOnboarding.tsx** — Removed page-load bouncer. Kept `navigate("/dashboard")` at lines 222 and 438 (legitimate post-onboarding navigation). - **src/components/settings/SecurityTab.tsx** — Removed `window.location.href = "/auth"` page-load bouncer. - **supabase/functions/kira-gpt/index.ts** and 6 other edge functions — Added `'https://creviamvp.vercel.app'` to ALLOWED_ORIGINS in all 7 functions. Deployed via `npx supabase functions deploy`. This fixed the Kira "hiccup" error. ```typescript const ALLOWED_ORIGINS = [ 'https://crevia.app', 'https://www.crevia.app', 'http://localhost:8080', 'http://localhost:5173', 'https://creviamvp.vercel.app', // ← added ]; ``` - **supabase/migrations/20260429000000_fix_handle_uniqueness.sql** — Pre-existing migration that fixed handle_new_user trigger uniqueness issue. Confirmed deployed and running correctly in production. 4. Errors and Fixes: - **Sign-in taking user back to login page**: Caused by OAuth redirect landing on `/auth` but old code not detecting it as an OAuth callback. Fixed by adding `hasOAuthCallback()` and `isProcessingOAuth` spinner state in Auth.tsx. - **Kira AI "hiccup" error on all messages**: Caused by all 7 edge functions missing `https://creviamvp.vercel.app` in ALLOWED_ORIGINS. Browser blocked every response. Fixed by patching and redeploying all 7 functions. - **New user signup redirecting back to login**: Multiple layered causes: 1. emailRedirectTo pointed to `/user-type-selection` which mapped to `` — fixed to `/auth` 2. No ProtectedRoute — users landed on /kira without session as "guest" — fixed by adding ProtectedRoute 3. ProtectedRoute race condition — INITIAL_SESSION firing null before session confirmed — fixed by using getSession() as primary check and only reacting to explicit SIGNED_IN/SIGNED_OUT/TOKEN_REFRESHED events 4. signUpData.session not checked — always navigated to /kira even without session — fixed with session check 5. Email confirmation ON + no session = "Check your inbox" screen rather than navigation - **"Database error saving new user"**: Investigated extensively. Root cause: NOT a database/migration issue. The Resend SMTP configuration is either incorrectly set up or failing during the signup email send, causing Supabase Auth to roll back the entire user creation transaction in newer Supabase versions. Evidence: zero email/password users in auth.users, zero orphaned users (full rollback), Google OAuth works fine (no SMTP needed). Cannot be fixed via migration — requires Supabase dashboard action. - **ProtectedRoute bouncing authenticated users on token refresh**: When access token expired, INITIAL_SESSION fired null before token refresh completed, causing redirect to /auth. Fixed by ignoring INITIAL_SESSION and using getSession() which handles token refresh internally before returning. - **Legacy manual auth bouncers causing OAuth race condition**: Per-page `if (!session) { navigate("/auth") }` checks fired before Supabase's 0.5s OAuth token exchange completed. Fixed by removing all page-load bouncers and relying on centralized ProtectedRoute. - **User feedback - commit rejection**: User explicitly rejected git commit attempts and stated "i want to push the codes myself". Adjusted to provide commit messages for user to run themselves. - **User feedback - careful about navigate() removal**: User warned "If a file has uses: 1, it's almost certainly the 'dumb bouncer'. If it has uses: 5, we need to be careful." Acknowledged and verified each remaining navigate() call before proceeding. 5. Problem Solving: **Solved:** - Google OAuth redirect loop — spinner shown during token exchange - Kira AI CORS blocking — all 7 edge functions now allow creviamvp.vercel.app - Auth redirect bouncing on all protected pages — centralized ProtectedRoute - Manual auth bouncers on 6 pages — all removed - ProtectedRoute race condition — deterministic getSession() + filtered onAuthStateChange - Signup UX confusion — dedicated "Check your inbox" screen with email address shown **Ongoing / Unresolved:** - "Database error saving new user" — diagnosed as Resend SMTP issue, requires dashboard fix (not code) - Email/password signup has never succeeded in production (0 email/password users in auth.users) 6. All User Messages: - Provided April 29 session summary context for Crevia including what was accomplished and what was blocked - "its still not functioning what might be the issue? When im creating a new user it takes me back again to login instead of kira" - "its working now back to kira Ai its not functioning well as required it returns a message saying 'sorry, i had a little hiccup! Could you try asking me again?'" - "i want to push the codes myself whats the commit message" - "when im creating an account it shouldnt take me back again to logging in i should now access kira Ai did we tackle this issue" - "first test and make sure if i rejected the changes and if yes we redo it again and make the creating account/logging in fully productive" - "its still repeating the same process, when i signin if i dont have an account, immediately after that it takes me back to the login page again can you review each line of the code and confirm where its not functioning and if yes correct it and make it fully productive since its only 2 hours to the launch" - "b4 the code was perfect and the app was functioning very well. Right now we have issues when signing in or creating an account. kindly dont change anything first review the codes and any mistake you find explain it first then i'll know what to proceed with" - "email confirmation is on" - "I have implemented a centralized ProtectedRoute.tsx component... Please scan my repository and: Remove all manual navigate('/auth') or navigate('/login') checks... Ensure these routes remain wrapped by the ProtectedRoute in App.tsx. Verify that ProtectedRoute correctly handles the 'loading' state..." - "Wait! A quick warning: Some of those navigate() uses might be good (like a button that says 'Go to Home'). Others are the bad ones (the 'dumb bouncers' checking for a session)." - "[E2EE] AppContent render logs... what does this mean" - "lets tackle this later can we deal with the frontend?" - "are we using backend for authentication or frontend for authentication?" - "and what about the session tokens? is this why im having a hard time when signing in a new user?" - "so the main issue here is the confirmation email being on?" - "i think b4 making any change i should discuss with my team members first" - "and what if i pay for supabase pro" - "ive already done all the necessary changes for Resend step by step and finished all the processes. So if i test now the app, i wont have the same issues?" - "I need to update my Supabase authentication flow... [full prompt about signUpData.session check and emailConfirmPending UI]" - "i had already done these changes? so it means ive already pushed them" - "else if (signUpData.session) { ... } this is the error from console: Signup error: Database error saving new user — this function is not creating a new user, what might be the main issue? explain first before changing anything" - "solve the migration of this particular error. Kindly don't touch anything else and also explain what you did" - "provide for me the whole context of what we've worked on and what is yet to be accomplished" 7. Pending Tasks: - **CRITICAL BLOCKER — Email/password signup broken**: "Database error saving new user" diagnosed as Resend SMTP misconfiguration in Supabase Auth settings. User must either: (A) Go to Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Providers → Email → toggle "Confirm email" OFF, OR (B) Verify Resend SMTP credentials (host: smtp.resend.com, port: 465, username: resend, password: Resend API key) are correctly saved in Supabase Project Settings → Auth → SMTP Settings. - **Recovery password E2E test**: Cannot be tested until email/password signup works. No code exists for this feature. - **Server-side feature gating**: Client-side limits exist but no server-side RPC/edge function guard. A user could bypass client-side checks. Needs a Postgres RPC or edge function enforcing plan limits before writes. - **Bank Integration (USD)**: Not started. - **Launch day**: Tomorrow (May 4, 2026). 8. The investigation used `npx supabase db query --linked` to probe the live database: - Confirmed handle_new_user function is the correct fixed version (WHILE loop for uniqueness) - Confirmed trigger is attached to auth.users (AFTER INSERT) - Confirmed all profiles columns have defaults or are nullable - Confirmed postgres role has rolbypassrls = true - Confirmed no orphaned users (zero auth.users rows without profiles) - Confirmed only 2 users exist, both Google OAuth (zero email/password signups ever succeeded) - Probed the exact INSERT the trigger performs — FK constraint confirmed working - Checked pg_trigger for conflicting triggers — only RI constraint triggers and our own - **Concluded**: Error is not from the database trigger. Wait - looking at the trigger more carefully:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, user_type, handle, email)
VALUES (
  NEW.id,
  COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_metadata->>'user_type', 'creator')::public.user_type,
  final_handle,
  NEW.email


);
```

content.thinking: Let me read the last migration to understand what it does:

content.content:
1	-- Fix handle_new_user trigger to gracefully handle duplicate handles.
2	-- The old version would crash with a unique constraint violation if the email
3	-- prefix was already taken (e.g. user signs up via Google then tries email/password).
4	-- Now it appends _1, _2, etc. until it finds a free handle.
5	
6	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
7	RETURNS trigger
8	LANGUAGE plpgsql
9	SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public
10	AS $$
11	DECLARE
12	  base_handle TEXT;
13	  final_handle TEXT;
14	  counter INTEGER := 0;
15	BEGIN
16	  -- Sanitise: lowercase, replace dots/special chars with underscores
17	  base_handle := LOWER(
18	    REGEXP_REPLACE(
19	      COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_metadata->>'handle', SPLIT_PART(NEW.email, '@', 1)),
20	      '[^a-z0-9_]', '_', 'g'
21	    )
22	  );
23	
24	  final_handle := base_handle;
25	
26	  -- Walk until we find a handle that isn't taken
27	  WHILE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE handle = final_handle) LOOP
28	    counter := counter + 1;
29	    final_handle := base_handle || '_' || counter;
30	  END LOOP;
31	
32	  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, user_type, handle, email)
33	  VALUES (
34	    NEW.id,
35	    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_metadata->>'user_type', 'creator')::public.user_type,
36	    final_handle,
37	    NEW.email
38	  );
39	
40	  RETURN NEW;
41	END;
42	$$;
43

content.text:
Understood. Here is the full context of what's been done and what was just found:

---

## What's been done (across both sessions)

**Auth architecture — done:**
- Created centralized `ProtectedRoute.tsx` wrapping all protected routes in `App.tsx`
- Removed 6+ manual per-page `if (!session) navigate('/auth')` bouncers
- Fixed OAuth redirect loop: `Auth.tsx` now shows a spinner (`isProcessingOAuth`) while Supabase exchanges the code, instead of flashing the login form
- Fixed email confirmation UX: `Auth.tsx` shows a "Check your inbox" screen when signup returns no session (confirmation ON)
- Set `emailRedirectTo` to `${window.location.origin}/auth` (was pointing to a non-existent route)

**Kira AI — done:**
- Added `https://creviamvp.vercel.app` to `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` in all 7 edge functions and redeployed. This fixed the CORS error that was returning "sorry, I had a little hiccup"

---

## Root cause of "Database error saving new user" — just discovered

The investigation this session found the **exact bug**:

- The migration `20260429000000_fix_handle_uniqueness.sql` (applied April 29 at 13:49) introduced a **column name typo** in the trigger
- It references `NEW.raw_user_metadata` but the actual PostgreSQL column in `auth.users` is `NEW.raw_user_meta_data` (extra underscore between `meta` and `data`)
- The trigger throws a runtime error → Supabase Auth rolls back the INSERT → "Database error saving new user"
- Both existing users (faruk.islam, creviaapp.tech) were created **before** that migration ran, which is why they work fine
- No new email/password or even new Google OAuth user has succeeded since April 29

**The fix is one line per reference:** change `raw_user_metadata` → `raw_user_meta_data` in the trigger function (two occurrences).

---

## What remains before launch (May 4)

1. **Critical — deploy the trigger fix** (one migration, ~2 minutes)
2. **Test email/password signup end-to-end** after the fix
3. **Test Google OAuth with a fresh account** to confirm new OAuth users also work
4. Lead-to-Contract workflow — not started
5. Server-side feature gating — only client-side limits exist
6. Bank integration (USD) — not started

---

When you're ready, say the word and I'll deploy the trigger column name fix as a new migration.

input.content:
# Plan: Email Confirmation Flow — Status & Card Text Update

## Context

The user wants to know if the email confirmation flow works end-to-end, and wants to update the "check your inbox" card text. Email/password signup is currently working **directly** (no confirmation email) because email confirmation is **OFF** in Supabase. The user wants it ON so users receive a confirmation email, and for the confirmation link to redirect to Kira.

---

## Current State (confirmed by database + code review)

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Email/password signup | ✅ Working (6 users total, 2 email/password) |
| Email confirmation | ❌ OFF — users go directly to Kira, no email sent |
| Confirmation link redirect to Kira | ✅ Already coded correctly |
| "Check your inbox" card | ✅ Already coded — only text needs changing |
| Trigger (handle_new_user) | ✅ Working (profiles created for email/pw users) |

---

## How the confirmation redirect already works (no code change needed)

When a user clicks the confirmation link in their email:
1. They land on `/auth?token_hash=abc123&type=email`
2. `hasOAuthCallback()` detects the `token_hash` param → sets `isProcessingOAuth = true`
3. A spinner shows: "Signing you in…"
4. Supabase automatically processes the token in the background
5. `onAuthStateChange` fires `SIGNED_IN` → `navigate("/kira", { replace: true })`

**This flow is already correctly coded. No changes required.**

---

## What needs to change

### Code change — Auth.tsx: Update the card text

**File:** `src/pages/Auth.tsx` (lines 289–313)

Current text:
- Title: `"Check your inbox"`
- Body: `"We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click the link in your email to activate your account and access Kira. Check your spam folder if you don't see it."`

Proposed new text:
- Title: `"Confirm your email"`
- Body: `"An email was sent to {email}. Kindly click the link in the email to confirm your account and get access to Kira. Check your spam folder if you don't see it."`

### Dashboard action (user must do this) — Turn email confirmation ON

The card will never show unless this is done:

1. Go to **Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Providers → Email**
2. Toggle **"Confirm email"** → ON
3. Save

Once ON:
- New signups will receive a confirmation email
- The card will be shown to new users
- Clicking the link takes them to Kira (already works)

> If Resend SMTP is configured in Project Settings → Auth → SMTP Settings, emails will come from your custom domain. If not, Supabase's default SMTP sends them (limited to ~3/hour on free plan).

---

## What I will change (code only)

1. **`src/pages/Auth.tsx`** — Update the `emailConfirmPending` card:
   - Change h1 from "Check your inbox" → "Confirm your email"
   - Change body text to: "An email was sent to `{pendingEmail}`. Kindly click the link in the email to confirm your account and get access to Kira."
   - Keep the "Back to sign in" button unchanged
   - Keep the spam folder note

## Verification

After the code change and after toggling confirmation ON in Supabase:
1. Sign up with a new email/password
2. The card should appear with the new text
3. Check email inbox → click the link
4. Should land on spinner at `/auth` → automatically navigate to `/kira`

They're saying that when they click the confirmation link from their email, it should automatically navigate to Kira - which it already does (via the token_hash detection). The "Back to sign in" button is just a fallback in case they want to go back to the login form without confirming.

The user seems to be confused - they think the "Back to sign in" button is what navigates them to Kira. But actually, the confirmation happens when they click the EMAIL LINK, not when they click "Back to sign in".


So the "Back to sign in" button is really just an escape hatch - it lets users cancel the confirmation process and return to the login form if they change their mind, while the actual navigation to Kira happens automatically when they click the email link with the token_hash parameter.

Wait, I'm re-reading what they said more carefully. They're saying the page *should* navigate to Kira automatically when they click the confirmation link from the email - which is exactly what's supposed to happen. So maybe they're questioning why the "Back to sign in" button exists at all, or wondering if it should be replaced with something more useful like a "Resend confirmation email" option instead.

Actually, thinking through the flow: when they click the email link, it goes to `/auth` with a token, which should process the confirmation and redirect to Kira in that same tab. The confirmation card page shouldn't even be visible anymore once the token is processed. So either the redirect isn't working properly, or they're confused about what should happen when clicking the email link versus what happens if they just stay on the confirmation card page. The "Back to sign in" button serves as an escape hatch for users who want to switch accounts or resend the confirmation email, but the real issue is whether the confirmation card should auto-update when the email link is clicked in a different tab—and currently it doesn't, since the navigation only happens when the link opens in the same browser window. The "Back to sign in" button is just an escape hatch that closes the confirmation card and returns to the login form—it doesn't interfere with the email link flow at all. When users click the email link, they'll navigate to Kira whether they open it in the same tab or a new one, so the main flow works fine. I should ask if they want to keep it, remove it for simplicity, or replace it with something like "Resend confirmation email."

content.text:
The "Back to sign in" button is just an escape hatch — if a user decides not to confirm right now and wants to log in with a different account, they can hit it. It has nothing to do with the email link flow.

The email link flow already works the way you described:
- User sees the confirmation card → checks their email → clicks the link → the browser opens `/auth?token_hash=...` → spinner shows → automatically navigates to Kira

The button is completely separate from that. It just closes the card and shows the login form again — nothing more.

Since you don't need it, the two sensible options are:

1. **Remove it entirely** — card just shows the message with no button (cleaner, no confusion)
2. **Replace it with "Resend confirmation email"** — a more useful action in case the email didn't arrive

content.thinking:
I found the issue! In AppLayout.tsx (lines 22-26):

```javascript
const appTheme = localStorage.getItem("app-theme");
if (appTheme) {
  setTheme(appTheme);
}
```

This reads from `localStorage.getItem("app-theme")` and applies it on every load. The `ThemeToggle` component sets both `"app-theme"` and `"theme"` in localStorage when a user changes theme.

But the main issue with "system mode" being the default: `defaultTheme="system"` in ThemeProvider means new users start with system mode. 

The problem is that if `"app-theme"` was previously set to "dark" or "light" from earlier testing, it'll override the system default. Plus, there's redundancy here—`next-themes` already manages the `"theme"` key in localStorage, so AppLayout reading from a separate `"app-theme"` key and calling `setTheme()` is duplicating what `next-themes` handles automatically. For the user's request that the app should default to system mode, I think they want any new user to start in system mode, and the stored preference should only apply if it was explicitly set by the user. defaultTheme="system"` handles this for new users

But the `"app-theme"` localStorage key in AppLayout might be persisting old preferences. Also, the `storageKey="theme"` in ThemeProvider is redundant with `"app-theme"`.

Actually, the real question is: does the user's browser currently have `localStorage["app-theme"]` or `localStorage["theme"]` set to "dark" or "light"? If so, it will persist that.

The user's request seems to be:
1. Make the app default to system mode (for new users - already done)
2. Users can manually change it (ThemeToggle already does this)

I don't think we need to change anything for the system mode requirement since `defaultTheme="system"` is already set. The ThemeToggle already supports system/light/dark.

However, there's a potential issue: the `"app-theme"` key in AppLayout might be reading from localStorage and overriding the system preference through `setTheme()`. This could be the source of the problem—if the user previously set a theme, that stored value would take precedence over the system default. I should check whether AppLayout is interfering with the next-themes configuration or if there's a way to clear that localStorage key so the system preference can take effect. The user might be seeing dark mode because their browser has a leftover `"app-theme"` value from previous testing or sessions, even though new users should default to system mode. Let me clarify what's actually working correctly and address what might be confusing them.
